seo_title: CAX-Agent: A Lightweight Agent Harness for Reliable APDL Automation
meta_description: CAX-Agent is a lightweight agent harness that addresses practical reliability challenges in large language models deployed for MAPDL finite-element simulation.
body_html:
The CAX-Agent paradigm addresses practical reliability challenges in large language models deployed for MAPDL finite-element simulation by inserting domain-specific orchestration middleware that manages tool lifecycles, workflow state, and recovery escalation.

Three Layers of Execution

  • LLM service: provides the computational power for the simulation
  • agent harness: manages tool lifecycles, workflow state, and recovery escalation
  • solver backend: performs the actual computation

Recovery Strategies Evaluated

Strategy Completion Rate Task Score Total Score Zero-Intervention Rate
No Recovery 0.6933 2.74/4 5.60/10 0.00
Rule Only 0.7733 3.17/4 7.03/10 0.00
Model Only 0.9267 3.59/4 9.16/10 0.84

Effectiveness of Model Only Strategy

Model_only achieves the best completion rate (0.9267), task score (3.59/4), total score (9.16/10), and zero-intervention rate (0.84), outperforming rule_only (0.7733, 3.17/4, 7.03/10, 0.00) and no_recovery (0.6933, 2.74/4, 5.60/10, 0.00) with large effect sizes (Cliff's delta = 0.81-0.87).

Limitations of Benchmark

The benchmark uses deliberately simple geometries to isolate recovery-policy effects; we discuss the scope of these findings and directions for broader validation.

source.title: CAX-Agent: A Lightweight Agent Harness for Reliable APDL Automation
source.summary: Large language models deployed for MAPDL finite-element simulation face practical reliability challenges: without structured execution control, tool encapsulation, and fault recovery, outputs may be inconsistent and task failures are common. The Agent Harness paradigm addresses this by inserting domain-specific orchestration middleware that manages tool lifecycles, workflow state, and recovery escalation. This paper presents the architecture of CAX-Agent, a lightweight agent harness purpose-built for MAPDL automation, and empirically evaluates one of its core components -- the recovery policy.CAX-Agent organizes execution into three layers -- LLM service, agent harness, and solver backend -- with a recovery ladder that escalates from deterministic rule patching through model-driven regeneration to context enrichment and human intervention. We evaluate three recovery strategies (no_recovery, rule_only, and model_only) on 50 standard structural benchmarks with three repeated runs per strategy (450 case-runs total). Two independent human raters score task completion under blind conditions; inter-rater agreement is strong (quadratic weighted Cohen's kappa = 0.84, 96 percent of score pairs within one point). Model_only achieves the best completion rate (0.9267), task score (3.59/4), total score (9.16/10), and zero-intervention rate (0.84), outperforming rule_only (0.7733, 3.17/4, 7.03/10, 0.00) and no_recovery (0.6933, 2.74/4, 5.60/10, 0.00) with large effect sizes (Cliff's delta = 0.81-0.87). The benchmark uses deliberately simple geometries to isolate recovery-policy effects; we discuss the scope of these findings and directions for broader validation.
